entries.node_behavior: process.binding() exposes internal C++ bindings, allowing unrestricted access to Node internals.
entries.franken_behavior: process.binding() is disabled by default. Returns a structured error with policy-gate information. Available only in legacy-risky mode with explicit policy opt-in.
entries.rationale: process.binding() bypasses all security boundaries and trust controls. Disabling it by default aligns with the impossible-by-default security model. Legacy applications requiring this behavior must explicitly opt in via policy configuration.

entries.node_behavior: vm.runInNewContext() creates a new V8 context and executes arbitrary code with configurable sandbox.
entries.franken_behavior: vm.runInNewContext() executes under franken_engine's sandboxed execution model with mandatory capability restrictions. Full V8 context isolation semantics are not replicated; instead, franken_engine provides deterministic execution boundaries.
entries.rationale: V8 context isolation has known escape vectors. franken_engine's native sandboxing provides stronger guarantees. Behavioral differences are documented and produce divergence receipts at runtime.

entries.node_behavior: child_process.exec() launches shell commands with ambient process authority and inherits host shell semantics.
entries.franken_behavior: Shell execution is treated as an unsafe compatibility behavior. Migration tooling marks it for sandbox policy approval, and runtime compatibility gates keep unsafe shims blocked outside explicit legacy-risky policy.
entries.rationale: Ambient shell execution can bypass capability boundaries and undermine deterministic replay. The divergence is intentional: operators must review and approve this behavior instead of inheriting Node's ambient authority by default.

entries.node_behavior: child_process.spawn() starts arbitrary host processes with caller-selected executables, arguments, environment, and inherited descriptors.
entries.franken_behavior: Process spawning is reviewed as an unsafe compatibility surface and must pass sandbox/capability policy before it is treated as production behavior.
entries.rationale: Unreviewed process creation can widen filesystem, network, and process-control authority. The product keeps this divergence visible so migrations cannot silently preserve ambient host authority.

entries.node_behavior: eval() compiles and executes dynamic source text inside the current JavaScript context.
entries.franken_behavior: Dynamic code execution is blocked by default migration policy and must be rewritten or explicitly approved before compatibility shims may expose it.
entries.rationale: Dynamic code evaluation defeats static migration analysis, provenance tracking, and capability preflight. Blocking it by default preserves trust-native policy guarantees while leaving a documented compatibility escape path.

entries.node_behavior: Node.js can load native addons that execute compiled host code inside the process.
entries.franken_behavior: Native addon loading is treated as an unsafe compatibility divergence that requires explicit policy review and cannot be silently accepted by the default compatibility mode.
entries.rationale: Native addons can bypass JavaScript-level sandboxing and provenance checks. The ledger records this as a first-class divergence so release gates and migration reports keep native code risk visible.
